intro: Gireoudia lindleyana, also known as the 'Milk Flower', is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Pedaliaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Gireoudia lindleyana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Gireoudia lindleyana include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, particularly those related to inflammation and infection.
